skill.name: agent-orchestration-improve-agent
skill.description: Systematic improvement of existing agents through performance analysis, prompt engineering, and continuous iteration.
skill.summary: Optimize AI agent performance through data-driven analysis, prompt engineering, and structured testing workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 24 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation providing guidance on AI agent optimization methodology. Detected 'external commands' are markdown tool references, not actual shell execution. Detected 'cryptographic algorithms' are plain text describing performance improvements. No actual security risks present.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers systematically improve AI agent performance through data-driven analysis, prompt engineering, and structured testing workflows, enabling continuous agent optimization with measurable results.
actual_capabilities: Analyze agent performance metrics including task completion rate, response accuracy, and tool usage efficiency
Identify failure modes and patterns in agent behavior through systematic classification
Apply prompt engineering techniques including chain-of-thought enhancement and few-shot example optimization
Design and execute A/B testing frameworks to compare agent versions with statistical validation
Implement version control and staged rollout strategies with rollback capabilities for safe deployment
Establish continuous monitoring and improvement cycles for ongoing agent optimization
limitations: Does not create new agents from scratch - only optimizes existing implementations
Requires existing metrics, feedback data, or test cases to be effective
Cannot directly modify external AI model parameters or API configurations
Success depends on having sufficient test data for statistical significance

use_cases.title: Enterprise AI Team Agent Optimization
use_cases.description: Use systematic performance analysis and prompt engineering to improve customer service agent accuracy and response quality.
use_cases.target_user: AI/ML engineers and product teams managing production AI agents

use_cases.title: Developer Workflow Enhancement
use_cases.description: Apply structured testing and A/B comparison to optimize code generation agents for specific programming languages or frameworks.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using Claude or Codex for code assistance

use_cases.title: Research-Based Agent Iteration
use_cases.description: Leverage comprehensive evaluation metrics and human evaluation protocols to research agent behavior improvements.
use_cases.target_user: AI researchers studying agent performance and optimization techniques

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the performance of my agent by reviewing its recent interactions. Identify the top 3 failure modes and suggest prompt engineering improvements for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have access to agent conversation logs and need quick improvement recommendations

prompt_templates.title: Prompt Optimization Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply chain-of-thought enhancement and few-shot example optimization to improve my agent's reasoning capabilities. Current prompt focus: [describe current prompt]. Target improvement area: [describe area].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to enhance specific aspects of agent reasoning or output quality

prompt_templates.title: A/B Test Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an A/B test framework to compare my current agent version with an improved version. Include test categories, sample size requirements, and statistical significance criteria.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing to validate agent improvements through controlled testing

prompt_templates.title: Staged Rollout Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a staged rollout plan for deploying my improved agent version. Include alpha, beta, and canary release stages with monitoring criteria and rollback triggers.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing to deploy agent changes to production with safety measures

output_examples.input: Analyze my customer support agent performance from the last 30 days
output_examples.output:
Performance Analysis Report:
- Task Success Rate: 78% (baseline: 72%)
- Top Failure Modes: Context loss (23%), Tool misuse (18%), Output format errors (15%)
- Recommended Priority: Address context loss through conversation summary prompts
- Expected Improvement: 8-12% success rate increase

output_examples.input: Design an A/B test for comparing prompt versions
output_examples.output:
A/B Test Framework:
- Test Set: 100 representative conversations
- Agent A: Original prompt
- Agent B: Enhanced with chain-of-thought
- Metrics: Success rate, avg response time, user satisfaction
- Required: 95% confidence level, minimum 100 samples per variant

best_practices: Always establish quantitative baseline metrics before making improvements to measure actual impact
Use staged rollouts with monitoring to catch issues early before full deployment
Implement rollback procedures before deploying any prompt changes to production
anti_patterns: Deploying prompt changes without regression testing on previously successful tasks
Skipping statistical validation and using insufficient test sample sizes
Ignoring user feedback patterns when prioritizing improvement areas

faq.question: What data do I need to start optimizing an agent?
faq.answer: You need baseline performance metrics, representative conversation examples, and ideally user feedback or correction patterns. The skill works best with at least 30 days of interaction data.

faq.question: Can this skill create a new agent from scratch?
faq.answer: No. This skill optimizes existing agents. For creating new agents, look for skills focused on agent scaffolding or initial prompt design.

faq.question: How long does agent optimization take?
faq.answer: A complete optimization cycle typically takes 2-4 weeks: 1 week for analysis, 1 week for prompt improvements, and 1-2 weeks for testing and validation.

faq.question: What metrics should I track for agent performance?
faq.answer: Key metrics include task completion rate, response accuracy, tool usage efficiency, response latency, token consumption, and user satisfaction scores.

faq.question: How do I know if my agent improvements are working?
faq.answer: Use A/B testing with statistical significance (95% confidence). Compare success rates, user corrections, and satisfaction scores between original and improved versions.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The optimization methodology works with any Claude agent. The prompts and frameworks are tool-agnostic and focus on improving agent behavior patterns.
